status.description: The registration status of the account.
The status of the Amazon Web Services account.

delegations.description: The list of delegations that the GetDelegations API returned.

nextToken.description: The pagination token that's used to fetch the next set of results.

id.description: The unique identifier for the delegation.

assessmentName.description: The name of the associated assessment.

assessmentId.description: The unique identifier for the assessment.

status.description: The current status of the delegation.

roleArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the IAM role.

creationTime.description: Specifies when the delegation was created.

controlSetName.description: Specifies the name of the control set that was delegated for review.

insights.description: The analytics data that the GetInsights API returned.

activeAssessmentsCount.description: The number of active assessments in Audit Manager.

noncompliantEvidenceCount.description: The number of compliance check evidence that Audit Manager classified as non-compliant on the lastUpdated date. This includes evidence that was collected from Security Hub with a Fail ruling, or collected from Config with a Non-compliant ruling.

compliantEvidenceCount.description: The number of compliance check evidence that Audit Manager classified as compliant on the lastUpdated date. This includes evidence that was collected from Security Hub with a Pass ruling, or collected from Config with a Compliant ruling.

inconclusiveEvidenceCount.description:
The number of evidence without a compliance check ruling. Evidence is inconclusive when the associated control uses Security Hub or Config as a data source but you didn't enable those services. This is also the case when a control uses a data source that doesn’t support compliance checks (for example: manual evidence, API calls, or CloudTrail).

If evidence has a compliance check status of not applicable, it's classed as inconclusive in Insights data.

assessmentControlsCountByNoncompliantEvi.description: The number of assessment controls that collected non-compliant evidence on the lastUpdated date.

totalAssessmentControlsCount.description: The total number of controls across all active assessments.

lastUpdated.description: The time when the cross-assessment insights were last updated.

adminAccountId.description: The identifier for the administrator account.

serviceMetadata.description: The metadata that's associated with the Amazon Web Services service.

name.description: The name of the Amazon Web Services service.

displayName.description: The display name of the Amazon Web Services service.

description.description: The description of the Amazon Web Services service.

category.description: The category that the Amazon Web Services service belongs to, such as compute, storage, or database.

assessmentReports.description: The list of assessment reports that the ListAssessmentReports API returned.

nextToken.description: The pagination token that's used to fetch the next set of results.

name.description: The name of the assessment report.

complianceType.description: The name of the compliance standard that's related to the assessment, such as PCI-DSS.

roles.description: The roles that are associated with the assessment.

controlDomainInsights.description: The control domain analytics data that the ListControlDomainInsights API returned.

nextToken.description: The pagination token that's used to fetch the next set of results.

id.description: The unique identifier for the control domain. Audit Manager supports the control domains that are provided by Amazon Web Services Control Catalog. For information about how to find a list of available control domains, see ListDomains in the Amazon Web Services Control Catalog API Reference.

controlsCountByNoncompliantEvidence.description: The number of controls in the control domain that collected non-compliant evidence on the lastUpdated date.

totalControlsCount.description: The total number of controls in the control domain.

evidenceInsights.description: A breakdown of the compliance check status for the evidence that’s associated with the control domain.

noncompliantEvidenceCount.description: The number of compliance check evidence that Audit Manager classified as non-compliant. This includes evidence that was collected from Security Hub with a Fail ruling, or collected from Config with a Non-compliant ruling.

compliantEvidenceCount.description: The number of compliance check evidence that Audit Manager classified as compliant. This includes evidence that was collected from Security Hub with a Pass ruling, or collected from Config with a Compliant ruling.

inconclusiveEvidenceCount.description:
The number of evidence that a compliance check ruling isn't available for. Evidence is inconclusive when the associated control uses Security Hub or Config as a data source but you didn't enable those services. This is also the case when a control uses a data source that doesn’t support compliance checks (for example, manual evidence, API calls, or CloudTrail).

If evidence has a compliance check status of not applicable in the console, it's classified as inconclusive in EvidenceInsights data.

lastUpdated.description: The time when the control domain insights were last updated.

nextToken.description: The pagination token that's used to fetch the next set of results.

id.description: The unique identifier for the notification.

assessmentName.description: The name of the related assessment.

controlSetId.description: The identifier for the control set.

controlSetName.description: Specifies the name of the control set that the notification is about.

eventTime.description: The time when the notification was sent.

status.description: The status of the account registration request.

snsTopic.description: The designated Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS) topic.

defaultAssessmentReportsDestination.description: The default S3 destination bucket for storing assessment reports.

destinationType.description: The destination type, such as Amazon S3.

destination.description: The destination bucket where Audit Manager stores assessment reports.

evidenceFinderEnablement.description: The current evidence finder status and event data store details.

eventDataStoreArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the CloudTrail Lake event data store that’s used by evidence finder. The event data store is the lake of evidence data that evidence finder runs queries against.

enablementStatus.description:
The current status of the evidence finder feature and the related event data store.

  • ENABLE_IN_PROGRESS means that you requested to enable evidence finder. An event data store is currently being created to support evidence finder queries.

  • ENABLED means that an event data store was successfully created and evidence finder is enabled. We recommend that you wait 7 days until the event data store is backfilled with your past two years’ worth of evidence data. You can use evidence finder in the meantime, but not all data might be available until the backfill is complete.

  • DISABLE_IN_PROGRESS means that you requested to disable evidence finder, and your request is pending the deletion of the event data store.

  • DISABLED means that you have permanently disabled evidence finder and the event data store has been deleted. You can't re-enable evidence finder after this point.

backfillStatus.description:
The current status of the evidence data backfill process.

The backfill starts after you enable evidence finder. During this task, Audit Manager populates an event data store with your past two years’ worth of evidence data so that your evidence can be queried.

  • NOT_STARTED means that the backfill hasn’t started yet.

  • IN_PROGRESS means that the backfill is in progress. This can take up to 7 days to complete, depending on the amount of evidence data.

  • COMPLETED means that the backfill is complete. All of your past evidence is now queryable.

error.description: Represents any errors that occurred when enabling or disabling evidence finder.

deregistrationPolicy.description: The deregistration policy for your Audit Manager data. You can use this attribute to determine how your data is handled when you deregister Audit Manager.

deleteResources.description:
Specifies which Audit Manager data will be deleted when you deregister Audit Manager.

  • If you set the value to ALL, all of your data is deleted within seven days of deregistration.

  • If you set the value to DEFAULT, none of your data is deleted at the time of deregistration. However, keep in mind that the Audit Manager data retention policy still applies. As a result, any evidence data will be deleted two years after its creation date. Your other Audit Manager resources will continue to exist indefinitely.

defaultExportDestination.description: The default S3 destination bucket for storing evidence finder exports.

destinationType.description: The destination type, such as Amazon S3.

destination.description: The destination bucket where Audit Manager stores exported files.

errors.description: A list of errors that the BatchAssociateAssessmentReportEvidence API returned.

delegations.description: The delegations that are associated with the assessment.

errors.description: A list of errors that the BatchCreateDelegationByAssessment API returned.

id.description: The unique identifier for the delegation.

assessmentName.description: The name of the assessment that's associated with the delegation.

assessmentId.description: The identifier for the assessment that's associated with the delegation.

roleArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the IAM role.

roleType.description:
The type of customer persona.

In CreateAssessment, roleType can only be PROCESS_OWNER.

In UpdateSettings, roleType can only be PROCESS_OWNER.

In BatchCreateDelegationByAssessment, roleType can only be RESOURCE_OWNER.

lastUpdated.description: Specifies when the delegation was last updated.

controlSetId.description: The identifier for the control set that's associated with the delegation.

comment.description: The comment that's related to the delegation.

manualEvidence.description: Manual evidence that can't be collected automatically by Audit Manager.

evidence.description: The evidence that the GetEvidence API returned.

dataSource.description: The data source where the evidence was collected from.

evidenceAwsAccountId.description: The identifier for the Amazon Web Services account.

time.description: The timestamp that represents when the evidence was collected.

eventSource.description: The Amazon Web Services service that the evidence is collected from.

eventName.description: The name of the evidence event.

resourcesIncluded.description: The list of resources that are assessed to generate the evidence.

attributes.description: The names and values that are used by the evidence event. This includes an attribute name (such as allowUsersToChangePassword) and value (such as true or false).

iamId.description: The unique identifier for the user or role that's associated with the evidence.

complianceCheck.description:
The evaluation status for automated evidence that falls under the compliance check category.

  • Audit Manager classes evidence as non-compliant if Security Hub reports a Fail result, or if Config reports a Non-compliant result.

  • Audit Manager classes evidence as compliant if Security Hub reports a Pass result, or if Config reports a Compliant result.

  • If a compliance check isn't available or applicable, then no compliance evaluation can be made for that evidence. This is the case if the evidence uses Config or Security Hub as the underlying data source type, but those services aren't enabled. This is also the case if the evidence uses an underlying data source type that doesn't support compliance checks (such as manual evidence, Amazon Web Services API calls, or CloudTrail).

This is also the case if the evidence uses an underlying data source type that doesn't support compliance checks (such as manual evidence, Amazon Web Services API calls, or CloudTrail).

enablementStatus.description:
The current status of the evidence finder feature and the related event data store.

  • ENABLE_IN_PROGRESS means that you requested to enable evidence finder. An event data store is currently being created to support evidence finder queries.

  • ENABLED means that an event data store was successfully created and evidence finder is enabled. We recommend that you wait 7 days until the event data store is backfilled with your past two years’ worth of evidence data. You can use evidence finder in the meantime, but not all data might be available until the backfill is complete.

  • DISABLE_IN_PROGRESS means that you requested to disable evidence finder, and your request is pending the deletion of the event data store.

  • DISABLED means that you have permanently disabled evidence finder and the event data store has been deleted. You can't re-enable evidence finder after this point.

backfillStatus.description:
The current status of the evidence data backfill process.

The backfill starts after you enable evidence finder. During this task, Audit Manager populates an event data store with your past two years’ worth of evidence data so that your evidence can be queried.

  • NOT_STARTED means that the backfill hasn’t started yet.

  • IN_PROGRESS means that the backfill is in progress. This can take up to 7 days to complete, depending on the amount of evidence data.

  • COMPLETED means that the backfill is complete. All of your past evidence is now queryable.

error.description: Represents any errors that occurred when enabling or disabling evidence finder.

deregistrationPolicy.description: The deregistration policy for your Audit Manager data. You can use this attribute to determine how your data is handled when you deregister Audit Manager.

deleteResources.description:
Specifies which Audit Manager data will be deleted when you deregister Audit Manager.

  • If you set the value to ALL, all of your data is deleted within seven days of deregistration.

  • If you set the value to DEFAULT, none of your data is deleted at the time of deregistration. However, keep in mind that the Audit Manager data retention policy still applies. As a result, any evidence data will be deleted two years after its creation date. Your other Audit Manager resources will continue to exist indefinitely.

defaultExportDestination.description: The default S3 destination bucket for storing evidence finder exports.

destinationType.description: The destination type, such as Amazon S3.

destination.description: The destination bucket where Audit Manager stores exported files.

controlInsightsByAssessment.description: The assessment control analytics data that the ListAssessmentControlInsightsByControlDomain API returned.

nextToken.description: The pagination token that's used to fetch the next set of results.

id.description: The unique identifier for the assessment control.

evidenceInsights.description: A breakdown of the compliance check status for the evidence that’s associated with the assessment control.

noncompliantEvidenceCount.description: The number of compliance check evidence that Audit Manager classified as non-compliant. This includes evidence that was collected from Security Hub with a Fail ruling, or collected from Config with a Non-compliant ruling.

compliantEvidenceCount.description: The number of compliance check evidence that Audit Manager classified as compliant. This includes evidence that was collected from Security Hub with a Pass ruling, or collected from Config with a Compliant ruling.

inconclusiveEvidenceCount.description:
The number of evidence that a compliance check ruling isn't available for. Evidence is inconclusive when the associated control uses Security Hub or Config as a data source but you didn't enable those services. This is also the case when a control uses a data source that doesn’t support compliance checks (for example, manual evidence, API calls, or CloudTrail).

If evidence has a compliance check status of not applicable in the console, it's classified as inconclusive in EvidenceInsights data.

controlSetName.description: The name of the control set that the assessment control belongs to.

lastUpdated.description: The time when the assessment control insights were last updated.

assessmentFrameworkShareRequests.description: The list of share requests that the ListAssessmentFrameworkShareRequests API returned.

nextToken.description: The pagination token that's used to fetch the next set of results.

id.description: The unique identifier for the share request.

frameworkId.description: The unique identifier for the shared custom framework.

frameworkName.description: The name of the custom framework that the share request is for.

frameworkDescription.description: The description of the shared custom framework.

status.description: The status of the share request.

sourceAccount.description: The Amazon Web Services account of the sender.

destinationAccount.description: The Amazon Web Services account of the recipient.

destinationRegion.description: The Amazon Web Services Region of the recipient.

expirationTime.description: The time when the share request expires.

creationTime.description: The time when the share request was created.

lastUpdated.description: Specifies when the share request was last updated.

comment.description: An optional comment from the sender about the share request.

standardControlsCount.description: The number of standard controls that are part of the shared custom framework.

customControlsCount.description: The number of custom controls that are part of the shared custom framework.

complianceType.description: The compliance type that the shared custom framework supports, such as CIS or HIPAA.

type.description: The framework type, such as a standard framework or a custom framework.

complianceType.description: The compliance type that the new custom framework supports, such as CIS or HIPAA.

lastUpdatedAt.description: The time when the framework was most recently updated.

id.description: The unique identifier for the control domain. Audit Manager supports the control domains that are provided by Amazon Web Services Control Catalog. controlsCountByNoncompliantEvidence.description: The number of controls in the control domain that collected non-compliant evidence on the lastUpdated date.

totalControlsCount.description: The total number of controls in the control domain.

evidenceInsights.description: A breakdown of the compliance check status for the evidence that’s associated with the control domain.

compliantEvidenceCount.description: The number of compliance check evidence that Audit Manager classified as compliant. This includes evidence that was collected from Security Hub with a Pass ruling, or collected from Config with a Compliant ruling.

inconclusiveEvidenceCount.description:
The number of evidence that a compliance check ruling isn't available for. Evidence is inconclusive when the associated control uses Security Hub or Config as a data source but you didn't enable those services. This is also the case when a control uses a data source that doesn’t support compliance checks (for example, manual evidence, API calls, or CloudTrail).

If evidence has a compliance check status of not applicable in the console, it's classified as inconclusive in EvidenceInsights data.

assessment.description: The response object for the UpdateAssessment API. This is the name of the updated assessment.

arn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the assessment.

awsAccount.description: The Amazon Web Services account that's associated with the assessment.

id.description: The identifier for the Amazon Web Services account.

emailAddress.description: The email address that's associated with the Amazon Web Services account.

name.description: The name of the Amazon Web Services account.

id.description: The unique identifier for the assessment.

complianceType.description: The name of the compliance standard that's related to the assessment, such as PCI-DSS.

status.description: The overall status of the assessment.

assessmentReportsDestination.description: The destination that evidence reports are stored in for the assessment.

destination.description: The destination bucket where Audit Manager stores assessment reports.

scope.description: The wrapper of Amazon Web Services accounts and services that are in scope for the assessment.

awsAccounts.description: The Amazon Web Services accounts that are included in the scope of the assessment.

awsServices.description:
The Amazon Web Services services that are included in the scope of the assessment.

This API parameter is no longer supported. If you use this parameter to specify one or more Amazon Web Services services, Audit Manager ignores this input. Instead, the value for awsServices will show as empty.

delegations.description: The delegations that are associated with the assessment.

description.description: The description for the control set.

controls.description: The list of controls that's contained with the control set.

delegations.description: The delegations that are associated with the control set.

systemEvidenceCount.description: The total number of evidence objects that are retrieved automatically for the control set.

manualEvidenceCount.description: The total number of evidence objects that are uploaded manually to the control set.

The control data sources where Audit Manager collects evidence from.

This API parameter is no longer supported.

The Controls object returns a partial response when called through Framework APIs. For a complete Controls object, use GetControl.

createdAt.description: The time when the control was created.

tags.description: The tags associated with the control.

state.description: The state of the control. The END_OF_SUPPORT state is applicable to standard controls only. This state indicates that the standard control can still be used to collect evidence, but Audit Manager is no longer updating or maintaining that control.

signatureValid.description: Specifies whether the signature key is valid.

signatureAlgorithm.description: The signature algorithm that's used to code sign the assessment report file.

signatureDateTime.description: The date and time signature that specifies when the assessment report was created.

signatureKeyId.description: The unique identifier for the validation signature key.

validationErrors.description: Represents any errors that occurred when validating the assessment report.
